sevenDayForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, Category 3, moving west southwest offshore from the Pilbara coast and intensifying.
points: Tropical Cyclone Advices and Forecast Track Maps are being issued and should be referred to for more detailed information.
Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is forecast to continue moving west southwest, roughly parallel to the Pilbara coast, tonight and into Thursday.
By early Friday, Narelle is expected to start turning towards the south to southeast and is likely to impact the west coast during Friday and Saturday.

trackForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle (34U) continues to steadily intensify as it moves roughly parallel to the Pilbara coast.
trackForecast.discussion:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle lies north of Karratha and is tracking steadily to the west-southwest. The environment is favourable, and the system is steadily intensifying, with Category 4 expected by Thursday afternoon. A period of rapid intensification is likely during the next 30 hours, and the chance of Narelle reaching category 5 intensity while north of the west Pilbara coast is assessed as being around 30%.

There is high confidence in the track to the west-southwest during Thursday, roughly parallel to the Pilbara coast. The extent of gales and storm force winds south of Narelle is larger than normal. Gales are occurring over open water and will occasionally extend to near coastal parts even as the system remains offshore. The risk of sustained gales in coastal parts increases in the far west Pilbara as the system moves closer to the coast.

Storm force winds may begin to impact the Pilbara coast from Karratha westwards during Thursday morning.

On Friday, Narelle is expected to turn toward the south to southeast, most likely doing so offshore, just west of the North West Cape. Storm force winds are likely to extend over the western Pilbara and northern Gascoyne during this time, and hurricane force winds are likely over coastal areas. Narelle is expected to track further south down the WA west coast later Friday and move inland on Saturday.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: Upper bound figures off the Pilbara coast reflect a more rapid intensification, reaching category 5 intensity north of the west Pilbara coast and remaining intense for longer.
